quote: 1. The stock doesn't know you own it. You feel emotional about a stock, but the stock doesn't feel anything about you.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 2. In this business, if you're good, you're right six times out of ten. You're never going to be right nine times out of ten.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 3. Far more money has been lost by investors preparing for corrections, or trying to anticipate corrections, than has been lost in corrections themselves.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 4. Know what you own, and know why you own it.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 5. Go for a business that any idiot can run — because eventually, any idiot probably is going to run it.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 6. Long shots almost never pay off.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 7. You should be able to explain why you own a stock in such simple terms that a fifth grader could understand it.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 8. Numbers are nice, but the basic story line is what matters.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 9. Owning stocks is like having children — don't get involved with more than you can handle.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 10. The best stock to buy may be the one you already own.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 11. Investing without research is like playing stud poker and never looking at the cards.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 12. Time is on your side when you own shares of superior companies.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 13. In the long run, it's not just how much money you make that will determine your future prosperity. It's how much of that money you put to work by saving it and investing it.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 14. Everyone has the brainpower to make money in stocks. Not everyone has the stomach.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 15. Understanding the story of a stock you own or intend to buy is as basic as understanding why your kid needs a shot.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 16. The person that turns over the most rocks wins the game.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 17. Absent a lot of surprises, stocks are relatively predictable over twenty years. As to whether they're going to be higher or lower in two or three years, you might as well flip a coin to decide.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 18. If you like the store, chances are you'll love the stock.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 19. Never invest in any idea you can't illustrate with a crayon.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 20. Selling your winners and holding your losers is like cutting the flowers and watering the weeds.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 21. Gentlemen who prefer bonds don't know what they're missing.
speaker: Peter Lynch
